meta.description: A positioned slot in a grid.

row.description: 1-based row position. Row 1 is the first data row (not a header — headers are column labels in the grid definition). There is no upper bound enforced by the spec; the grid renders rows up to the highest occupied row.

column.description: Column reference. For indexed grids (gridPayload.columns is null): letter-index matching Excel convention — 'A' through 'Z', then 'AA', 'AB'… For named-column grids: the column key from gridPayload.columns[].key. The adapter validates that this key exists in the grid definition when the grid is in named-column mode.

colSpan.description: Number of columns this cell spans. 1 means no merge (default). When colSpan > 1, the cell visually occupies columns [column … column + colSpan - 1]. The adapter does not create cell items for the covered columns — the spanning cell item is the authority for that rectangle. Null is equivalent to 1.

rowSpan.description: Number of rows this cell spans. 1 means no merge (default). When rowSpan > 1, the cell visually occupies rows [row … row + rowSpan - 1]. As with colSpan, no additional cell items are created for covered positions. Null is equivalent to 1.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
